creative_brief: Hero image: a parent and child at a farm hub pickup, brown paper bags with visible fresh produce, handwritten farm name tags visible on packaging. Warm natural light, no supermarket aesthetic. Earthy palette — greens, kraft browns, warm whites. Photography style: documentary-real, not staged.
headline: Do you actually know what's in your family's grocery bag?
subhead: Most of us don't. The label says 'Australian grown' but can't tell you which farm, which farmer, or what the land looked like after harvest. FarmThru changes that — and now you can own a piece of the change.
hero_copy: You've stood in the supermarket aisle, turned the packet over, and felt — nothing. No farm name. No story. No way to know if the soil that grew it was left better or worse. That quiet doubt? It's not paranoia. It's the cost of a supply chain built to hide things from you. FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store that strips out the warehouses, the wholesalers, the middlemen — and shows you exactly where your food comes from. Order online. Collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. Feed your family food you can actually trace to the paddock.

sections.heading: The doubt is real — and it starts with distance
sections.body: Supermarket supply chains move food an average of 2,400km before it reaches you. Along the way, it passes through wholesalers, warehouses, and distribution centres. Every step adds cost. Every step removes traceability. By the time it hits the shelf, the farmer's name is gone. FarmThru works differently. We partner directly with farms and suppliers across NSW — around 15 to 20 and growing. Bundarra Farm raises pork you can trace to the paddock. Collins Wild Caught hauls seasonal Australian seafood off the South Coast. Farmer Brown's eggs come from NSW. Paris Creek Farms in South Australia produces carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. These aren't anonymous producers. They're named people doing regenerative work — and when you shop with us, you know exactly who fed your family.

sections.heading: Rachel Ward didn't just talk about regenerative farming — she built one
sections.body: On her property in Kempsey, NSW, Rachel Ward runs a regenerative farm that puts soil health first. She's an actor turned farming advocate — and a key partner and supplier to FarmThru. Her conviction is simple: food should heal the land it comes from, not strip it bare. That's the principle behind every product on our shelves. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero unnecessary middlemen. Just a direct line from farm to our Brookvale hub to your kitchen table. Not a subscription box. No lock-in. No membership fees. You order when you want, collect when it suits you. Bulk buying keeps prices fair for families who commit to eating this way.

sections.heading: This isn't just a grocery store — it's a bet on a different food system
sections.body: Every dollar spent at a supermarket votes for the system that exists. Long supply chains. Squeezed farmers. Soil that gets worse each season. FarmThru is building the alternative: a farm-direct model where farmers are paid fairly, food arrives fresher, and regenerative practices are rewarded — not punished. We're now inviting everyday Australians to own a piece of it through our equity crowdfunding campaign. This isn't charity. It's co-ownership. You'd be investing in a real business with real risks — early-stage companies can fail, and your capital is at risk. But if you've ever wanted to vote for change with your wallet in a way that goes beyond the checkout, this is your chance to be a partner in what comes next.

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body:
1. Browse our online store — real food from named regenerative farms across NSW and beyond.
2. Order what you need, when you need it. No subscription. No lock-in.
3. Collect from our Brookvale hub (Unit 23, 10-18 Orchard Rd, Brookvale NSW 2100), Monday to Friday. Delivery also available to Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong.
4. Want to go further? Join our equity crowdfunding waitlist with a fully refundable $5 deposit. Be part of building a grocery store that shows you where your food comes from.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is conducting an equity crowdfunding campaign. Investing in early-stage companies carries significant risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are promised or guaranteed. This page is not financial advice. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances and read the offer document in full before making any investment decision.
